sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: West Ham United are in dismal form, having lost three of their last five matches and failing to secure a win in their last six games. In contrast, Newcastle United are riding a wave of confidence with three consecutive victories in their recent five outings, showcasing strong attacking intent and defensive solidity.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these sides, Newcastle have had the upper hand with a 1-0 victory at West Ham's London Stadium, while West Ham managed a 2-0 win at Newcastle. Historically, Newcastle have won three of the last five head-to-heads, suggesting a slight edge.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: West Ham are struggling offensively, ranking 18th in goals scored with just 7 goals, and defensively, conceding 20 goals (19th in the league). Newcastle, sitting 13th with 12 points, have a balanced record with 9 goals scored and 8 conceded, indicating a more cohesive unit.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Trends
sections.text: Newcastle's Bruno Guimarães and Harvey Barnes have been standout performers, with Barnes averaging a 7.38 Sofascore rating against West Ham. West Ham's defensive frailties, conceding first in their last six games, could be exploited by Newcastle's knack for scoring late, with 50% of their goals coming after the 76th minute.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Newcastle's superior form and West Ham's ongoing struggles, the Sofascore prediction of a 52% chance for an away win seems plausible. Expect Newcastle to capitalize on West Ham's defensive lapses, potentially securing a narrow victory in a match that could see both teams scoring based on historical trends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at the London Stadium between West Ham United and Newcastle United ended in a 1-1 stalemate, reflecting a competitive and evenly matched contest. Both teams showed moments of promise in attack, with goals from Jacob Murphy for Newcastle early in the game and Lucas Paquetá for West Ham later in the half. An unfortunate own goal by Sven Botman right at the end of the half leveled the scoreline. Both sides had their chances, with West Ham edging slightly in terms of shots on target, while Newcastle held a marginal advantage in possession and aerial duels.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: West Ham United demonstrated a strong presence in the final third with numerous entries and decent penalty area penetration, suggesting they’ve been able to create opportunities close to goal. Their duel-winning ability stood out, indicating a physical edge over Newcastle in ground battles. Newcastle United, on the other hand, matched West Ham’s final third presence but struggled more with ground duels. Their aerial dominance was notable, which helped them win key battles in the air and maintain a balanced share of possession.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. West Ham started with intent and maintained a prolonged spell of attacking pressure in the initial stages, pushing forward consistently. Newcastle responded with a sharp shift in momentum midway through the half, orchestrating a dangerous phase of play that challenged West Ham’s defense. Towards the closing minutes, West Ham regained control with significant attacking spells, culminating in their equalizer. However, Newcastle managed a late surge just before the break to keep the game tight.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player contributions
halftimeSections.text: For West Ham, Lucas Paquetá was the standout performer, not only scoring the equalizer but also contributing defensively with tackles and clearances. His overall influence in midfield has been pivotal. Jarrod Bowen and Crysencio Summerville also posed threats, with the latter getting shots on target. On Newcastle’s side, Jacob Murphy made an early impact with his goal and remained a constant danger in attack. Anthony Gordon showed flashes of brilliance, while Bruno Guimarães provided creativity with a key assist, despite picking up a yellow card.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: West Ham will need to capitalize more effectively on their penalty area entries and convert their final third presence into clearer scoring opportunities. Defensively, they must remain vigilant against Newcastle’s aerial strength. Newcastle, meanwhile, should focus on improving their ground duel success to gain more control in midfield. Their inability to win possession in the final third is a concern that could limit their attacking output if not addressed in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half promises to be an intriguing battle as both teams have shown they can hurt each other. West Ham will likely push to exploit their physical advantage and home crowd support at the London Stadium to take the lead. Newcastle, with their aerial prowess and ability to strike on the counter, will aim to disrupt West Ham’s rhythm and find gaps in defense. Given the attacking intent displayed by both sides, the game could see more goals, with West Ham slightly favored to edge it due to their first-half momentum late on.
